query: You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository. The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 441a9c92). ## Issue to Resolve Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem. The code below is comparable to your example: ``` // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right -> | val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null ``` The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code. Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` This is horrible code. For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line: ``` val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping ``` Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `CHANGELOG.md` - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.name: read

content.thinking:
**Diagnosing newline conflict in nullable function type**

The newline inserted before the inner function type's parameter list by wrapNullableType triggers parameter-list-wrapping's "Unnecessary newline before (" error; this suggests the formatting rules need adjustment to allow that newline inside nullable function types.**Refining detection of function types wrapped by nullable types**

The existing check misses cases where the function type is wrapped inside an extra parenthesized type, causing improper newline enforcement and false lint errors; adjusting detection to account for intermediate parentheses should fix erroneous parameter-list wrapping inside nullable function types.

**Considering engine directory**

I'm planning to check the engine directory next to explore relevant code for the task.

**Analyzing traversal order impact on formatting rules**

Checking whether newlines inserted by wrapping nullable types affect parameter list wrapping due to the depth-first traversal order and rule invocation sequence.**Identifying AST structure causes false negatives**

Noticing that the function type inside nullable parentheses involves an extra PARENTHESIZED_TYPE node causing the current check to miss nullable wrapping, suggesting the need to traverse ancestors more flexibly to detect nullable function types correctly.

conversation_history.name: read
read
conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.QUEST
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 9→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
10→
11→public class NullableTypeSpacingRule : StandardRule("nullable-type-spacing") {
12→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
13→        node: ASTNode,
14→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
15→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
16→    ) {
17→        node
18→            .takeIf { node.elementType == QUEST }
19→            ?.prevLeaf()
20→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
21→            ?.let { whiteSpaceBeforeQuest ->
22→                emit(whiteSpaceBeforeQuest.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
23→                if (autoCorrect) {
24→                    (whiteSpaceBeforeQuest as LeafPsiElement).rawRemove()
25→                }
26→            }
27→    }
28→}
29→
30→public val NULLABLE_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = NullableTypeSpacingRule().ruleId
31→

content.thinking:
**Evaluating parameter list wrapping logic**

I'm considering modifying the wrapping check to traverse upward through nullable types to skip parameter list wrapping for function types inside them, relying on nullable type wrapping to handle long lines—but I need to confirm this won't leave unwrapped long lines when function types themselves are lengthy but not exceeding nullable type thresholds.**Adjusting newline allowance before LPAR in nullable function types**

I'm clarifying that wrapping the outer nullable type inserts newlines around it but doesn't split inner parameters, so parameter list wrapping must still apply for long parameter lists; to avoid triggering "Unnecessary newline before (" errors, I'll update the LPAR handling to permit a newline before LPAR when it's the first parameter list child of a function type wrapped inside a nullable type.**Finalizing helper to allow newline before LPAR after nullable type LPAR**

I'm defining a precise helper to detect when an LPAR starts a function type parameter list immediately following the outer LPAR of a nullable type, so that wrapParameterInList can allow a newline before this LPAR without error, resolving the formatting conflict minimally and safely.

content.thinking:
**Verifying nullable type parent check**

The helper’s logic requiring the function type node’s parent to be NULLABLE_TYPE is sound because the AST shows FUNCTION_TYPE is a direct child of NULLABLE_TYPE, ensuring the skip condition for the error suppression applies correctly.**Confirming early return logic and test considerations**

The early return within the LPAR case correctly exits the function to skip further processing, and although tests aren’t required, adding new test files could verify the fix; for now, a minimal fix explaining allowance of a newline before "(" in nullable function types suffices.

content.text:
Summary:
- Allowed a newline before the function type parameter list when it directly follows the opening parenthesis of a nullable function type, preventing the spurious “Unnecessary newline before (” violation.

Details:
- Added a small guard in `wrapParameterInList` to skip the newline-removal check for the inner `(` of a nullable function type.
- Introduced `isStartOfFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType` helper to detect this specific scenario.

If you want, I can add a new test file to cover this case.
